extraction.one_line_summary: DEGENT Thomas won from a 4-rider breakaway sprint after FABBERPOOL and GIRMAY's chase group closed to 10 seconds with 2km remaining but couldn't bridge.
tactical_patterns: FABBERPOOL attacked at 46km on final circuit climb, splitting original 21-rider break and forcing selection before sustained moves.
DEGENT-VONHOOKER duo controlled final 3-4km of breakaway, allowing DEGENT to launch sprint with 200m remaining from protected position.
LOPEZ Trek-Segafredo forced to chase despite 4:06 GC buffer when GUILLAUMATA entered break, revealing conservative team GC defense threshold.
KEMNA attacked inside 10km despite 38-second deficit, but LOPEZ marked wheel directly rather than letting teammates control.

rider_signals.DEGENT Thomas: positive: first Grand Tour stage win in 3 years shows renewed breakaway finishing speed at age 35+
rider_signals.FABBERPOOL Mauro: positive: initiated decisive 46km attack on climb that shaped final selection, but couldn't match sprint speed
rider_signals.GIRMAY Biniam: negative: collected intermediate sprint points but failed to bridge 10-second gap in final 2km despite descent advantage
rider_signals.LOPEZ Juan Pedro: positive: marked KEMNA's late attack personally rather than relying on teammates, showing confidence and form pre-Blockhaus
rider_signals.GUILLAUMATA Guillaume: positive: infiltrated break at only 4:06 GC gap, forcing race leader's team to work and moved to 4th at 1:06
